message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We compared the ability of 2 commercial molecular amplification assays [
                  <jats:italic>Real</jats:italic>
                  Time SARS-CoV-2 on the
                  <jats:italic>m</jats:italic>
                  2000 (Abbott) and ID NOW™ COVID-19 (Abbott)] and a laboratory-developed test [modified CDC 2019-nCoV RT-PCR assay with RNA extraction by eMag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  (bioMérieux) and amplification on QuantStudio™ 6 or ABI 7500 Real-Time PCR System (Life Technologies)] to detect SARS-CoV-2 RNA in upper respiratory tract specimens. Discrepant results were adjudicated by medical record review. 200 nasopharyngeal swab specimens in viral transport medium (VTM) were collected from symptomatic patients between March 27 and April 9, 2020. Results were concordant for 167 specimens (83.5% overall agreement), including 94 positive and 73 negative specimens. The
                  <jats:italic>Real</jats:italic>
                  Time SARS-CoV-2 assay on the m2000 yielded 33 additional positive results, 25 of which were also positive by the modified CDC assay but not by the ID NOW™ COVID-19 assay. In a follow-up evaluation, 97 patients for whom a dry nasal swab specimen yielded negative results by the ID NOW™ COVID-19 assay had a paired nasopharyngeal swab specimen collected in VTM and tested by the
                  <jats:italic>Real</jats:italic>
                  Time SARS-CoV-2 assay; SARS-CoV-2 RNA was detected in 13 (13.4%) of these specimens. Medical record review deemed all discrepant results to be true positives. The ID NOW™ COVID-19 test was the easiest to perform and provided a result in the shortest time: as soon as 5 minutes for positive and 13 minutes for negative result. The
                  <jats:italic>Real</jats:italic>
                  Time SARS-CoV-2 assay on the m2000 detected more cases of COVID-19 infection than the modified CDC assay or the ID NOW™ COVID-19 test.
                </jats:p>
title: Comparison of two commercial molecular tests and a laboratory-developed modification of the CDC 2019-nCOV RT-PCR assay for the qualitative detection of SARS-CoV-2 from upper respiratory tract specimens

reference.unstructured: Bernard Stoecklin S , Rolland P , Silue Y , Mailles A , Campese C , Simondon A , Mechain M , Meurice L , Nguyen M , Bassi C , Yamani E , Behillil S , Ismael S , Nguyen D , Malvy D , Lescure FX , Georges S , Lazarus C , Tabai A , Stempfelet M , Enouf V , Coignard B , Levy-Bruhl D , Investigation T . 2020. First cases of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) in France: surveillance, investigations and control measures, January 2020. Euro Surveill 25.

First cases of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) in the WHO European Region, 24 January to 21 February 2020. Euro Surveill 25.

CLSI. 2008. User Protocol for Evaluation of Qualitative Test Performance; Approved Guideline-Second Edition. CLSI document EP12-A2. Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute, Wayne, PA.
